# Routine Reviews

### The community submitted 27 reviews to tell us what they like about Routine, what Routine can do better, and more.

Based on 27 reviews

Review Routine? [...] Based on 27 reviews

Review Routine?

Reviewers largely praise Routine as a polished daily planning tool that brings calendar, tasks, and notes together in a way that helps them stay organized, focused, and save time. Repeated highlights include strong design, intuitive workflow, time blocking, meeting notes, and fast keyboard-driven capture. Several users say it has become their main productivity app. The main caveat is maturity: reviewers mention bugs, rough edges in the user experience, missing features, and a weaker mobile experience, though many also note steady improvement and an active roadmap.

Just an awesome product. Very clean and poweful even though still early. I use Routine as my main productivity app for managing my time, tasks and also taking a few notes. I've been using the product for a year and it has helped me a lot for being a lot more focused.

Routine's approach is mind blowing and really well done. Great design, UX and feature set though some things are missing like Android, references etc. But everything seems to be on the roadmap. A few bugs remain as well but the team seems to be nearing a 1.0 version.

results.title: superwhisper Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
#### What's great

multi-language support (3)daily use (9)voice to text accuracy (8)customizable modes (5)

Report

 16 views

•1 review

SuperWhisper has been a game changer for me. I'm able to move much faster as I work by using my voice instead of typing. I've also discovered that I process concepts better when I verbalize them. Dictating my communications with SuperWhisper has allowed me to do that in ways that I couldn't when I was typing more. The AI integration completely negates the feeling I used to have when using traditional dictation tools (ahem, Siri). I don't have to worry about getting everything said perfectly, nor even worry about having perfect grammar. Neil, the developer, is very responsive, open to suggestions, and welcomes critical feedback.

#### What's great [...] #### What's great

AI integration (5)daily use (9)

Report

 19 views

•1 review

I use superwhisper daily in both my private life and while working. The biggest advantage is the flexibility it offers; you can choose the voice and language models that best suit your needs, and you program your own modes for different tasks you want to accomplish. Whether you want to write a short story, take notes, or need to draft a memo for work, superwhisper can be adapted to suit any situation. You can also teach it vocabulary that you use if it isn't part of everyday language, which increases the accuracy of the dictation. This adaptability is the greatest benefit of superwhisper, as it can tailored to your needs and effectively integrated into your daily life

#### What's great [...] 1. Simulate Keypress – Smarter Typing Automation The current Simulate Keypress feature is powerful, but with just a few tweaks, it could be even more effective: • Custom delay control between keypresses • Option to randomize keypress delays for more human-like typing • Ability to paste plain text that SuperWhisper then types out character by character (like an autotyper) These changes would be great for accessibility, automation, and productivity workflows. ⸻ 2. AI Assistant Mode – Context-Aware Voice Actions Imagine a mode where SuperWhisper becomes a lightweight AI voice assistant that: • Listens for spoken prompts or intent • Automatically executes contextual actions, like launching apps, typing commands, or navigating interfaces Even better: let it interact directly with onscreen
